_note: Curated list of recognizable players who are absent, omitted, or withdrawn from the 2026 tournament. This is a fan-facing editorial layer built on the local availability overlay.
caution: Use these cards to explain why a recognizable player is not involved, not to predict matchday lineups.
Every entry should show the reason and date so readers do not assume a player was simply overlooked by the site.
For deeper source trails, look up the matching teamCode in src/data/world-cup-availability.json.

players.reason: Ruptured ACL injury kept him out of the final World Cup squad.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is one of the Dutch attack's most recognizable young stars.

players.reason: He withdrew before the tournament and was replaced by Lutsharel Geertruida.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is a well-known Dutch defender and one of the first names casual fans notice when scanning the squad.

players.reason: He was left out of Uruguay's final World Cup squad.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is one of the defining strikers of the modern World Cup era.

players.reason: He was dropped from Iran's final squad.
players.whyFansKnowHim: For many casual viewers, he has long been one of the first attacking names associated with Iran.

players.reason: He was omitted from Ivory Coast's final World Cup squad.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is one of the best-known Ivorian names for fans who follow major European leagues.

players.reason: He was ruled out by injury after the final squad was named, and Austria did not call a replacement.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is one of Austria's most recognizable attacking midfield names.

players.reason: He was replaced because of injury before Scotland's opener.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is one of the better-known Scotland midfielders among Premier League and European club viewers.

players.reason: An ACL injury forced him out and Canada confirmed Jayden Nelson as the replacement.
players.whyFansKnowHim: He is one of the young Canadian names casual North American fans tend to recognize quickly.
